title: Massaman Roasted Norwegian Vegetable Flatbread
description: A light and flavorful Norwegian dish featuring Massaman paste and roasted vegetables, perfect for a date night. This ovo-vegetarian friendly recipe is made with long fermentation dough and cooked on a tava griddle.
instructions: Preheat the tava griddle to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the long fermentation dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Spread the Massaman paste evenly over the dough, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Arrange the chopped carrots, Brussels sprouts, and red onion on one half of the dough.
Fold the other half of the dough over the vegetables to form a half moon shape.
Brush the edges of the dough with olive oil and press gently to seal.
Place the flatbread on the preheated tava griddle and cook for 20 minutes, flipping halfway through.
While the flatbread is cooking, fry the eggs in a pan with a little olive oil until the whites are set and the yolks are cooked to desired doneness.
Once the flatbread is cooked, top it with the fried eggs, crumbly feta cheese, and a sprinkle of chopped fresh herbs.
Slice the flatbread into wedges and serve immediately.
